editionStmt.edition: Digital Critical Edition of the Chapter Summaries (Capitula) to Iosue in the Tours Bibles

publicationStmt.p:
Welcome to the digital critical edition project of the Capitula to the Octateuch in the Tours Bibles!
The editions are part of the PRIN 2022 PNRR project DOBiPS – Data Oriented Biblical Paratext Studies, awarded for the 2023–2025 period to the research units at the Universities of Udine (P.I. E. Colombi) and Cassino (P.I. R. Casavecchia), under the competitive PRIN PNRR call – National Recovery and Resilience Plan, Mission 4 "Education and Research", funded by the European Union’s NextGenerationEU programme (protocol no. P2022ZW4AW).
The Capitula prefixed to the Octateuch in the Tours Bibles have been encoded in XML/TEI and published using the software EVT (Edition Visualization Technology).
The editions were prepared by members of the Udine research unit: Emanuela Colombi, Margherita Filippozzi, and Silverio Franzoni.
Details of the encoding choices and related methodological reflections have been presented in the working paper: E. Colombi, M. Filippozzi, S. Franzoni, The Digital Critical Edition of the Capitula to the Octateuch in the Tours Bibles, DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.16761173.
Information on the individual editions is available by clicking the 'info' button at the top of the left-hand pane.

bibl.author: Donatien de Bruyne
bibl.title: Sommaires, divisions et rubriques de la Bible latine

bibl.author: Bernhard Bischoff
bibl.title: Katalog der festländischen Handschriften des neunten Jahrhunderts (mit Ausnahme der wisigotischen)
Heptateuchi partis posterioris versio Latina antiquissima e codice Lugdunensi. Version latine du Deutéronome, de Josué et des Juges antérieure à saint Jérôme...

bibl.editor: Monachi abbatiae pontificiae Sancti Hieronymi in Urbe
bibl.title: Biblia Sacra iuxta Latinam vulgatam versionem ad codicum fidem... Libri Iosue Iudicum Ruth ex interpretatione sancti Hieronymi cum praefatione et variis capitulorum seriebus

I + 423 folios

source.p: Fourth period (Abbot Vivian, 843-851) of the Turonian production according to Fischer's classification (p. 267).

I + 382 + I folios
height 495 mm width 350 mm

source.p: Fifth period (853-c.900) of the Turonian production according to Fischer's classification (p. 268).

listWit.witness: text of series A as printed by D. de Bruyne, Sommaires..., p. 42

tagsDecl.rendition: the digital reproduction is b/w, impossible to determine color

front.p:
This digital edition presents the reconstruction of the capitula of series A (according to Donatien de Bruyne's classification) that precede the Book of Iosue, in the textual form known from the Tours scriptorium (Φ).
This series consists of 33 capitula, of uneven length and corresponding to portions of the biblical text of varying extent.
As the analysis of the text and its comparison to both the Vulgate and the Vetus Latina (as transmitted by the second part of the Codex Lugdunensis: Lyon, BM, 403 + 1964, siglum VL 100; edition by U. Robert, 1900) demonstrate, this series is based on a Vulgate text of the book of Iosue. See, e.g.:
cap. 1 (Ios 1.5) fui A, Vulg. : eram VL 100;
cap. 2 (Ios 1.11) cibaria A, Vulg. : frumentum VL 100;
cap. 3 (Ios 2.1) viros exploratores Vulg. (exploratores A) : viros ad explorandum VL 100
cap. 8 (Ios 5.4) haec autem causa est secundae circumcisionis A, Vulg. : hoc autem modo purgauit Iesus filios Istrahel et hoc uerbo quo circumcisa est VL 100
cap. 10 (Ios 5.14) princeps exercitus Domini A, Vulg. : princeps militiae uirtutis Domini VL 100
cap. 13 (Ios 7.1) filii autem Israhel praevaricati sunt Vulg. (Praeuaricatio populi A) : et neglexerunt fili Istrahel VL 100
cap. 26 (Ios 18.1) congregatique sunt omnes filii israhel A, Vulg. : et congregata est omnis synagoga filiorum Istrahel VL 100 Each capitulum generally summarizes, with greater or lesser precision, the corresponding biblical passage, or at least mentions the main episode of that passage; sometimes the biblical text is reused more literally (see, e.g., capp. 1, 8, 20, 25, 26, 27).
It should be noted that, due to the tight binding of the manuscript, the chapter numbers in Tr (Troyes, Médiathèque Jacques-Chirac, MS 29) are almost never visible in the online digitisation which has been used for this edition.
In contrast to all the other Tours Bibles, P3 (Paris, BnF, lat. 47) attests de Bruyne’s series B (p. 43-45) rather than the series A.
Non-Turonensian production is represented here only by the Maurdramnus Bible (the book of Iosue is found in its second volume, now Amiens, BM, MS 7: Am2), since Tu (Tours, BM, 10) presents a peculiar series (although very close to de Bruyne's series A), which is labelled Λ(c) in the Benedictine edition of the Vulgate; this series has also been edited separately in the context of our project (see here). In order not to overburden the critical apparatus, its readings have not been reported here; in the edition of Tu's series, however, the differences with respect to the A series are indicated.
Any philological discussion of the A series in Tours Bibles is hindered by the fact that errors are few and of little significance. The witnesses do not form any consistent grouping; there may be some evidence that no later manuscript can derive from certain of them, which contain uncorrected errors that would have been difficult to detect and/or to correct without contamination. See, e.g.:
Am2: die omitted (cap. 32)
P4: transeat for transeant (cap. 2)
Mb2: unius diei for diei unius (cap. 17)
B2: magnitudinis (cap. 31) and die obitus sui (cap. 32) omitted; wrong numbering from cap. 18 on At cap. 19, the error dare for dari was probably not difficult to identify and correct; its occurrence in the two earliest witnesses, Am2 and Sg1, if not accidental, may suggest that it is an ancient error subsequently corrected in later manuscripts.
Throughout the edition, certain words are highlighted in different ways in order to signal noteworthy features. For technical reasons, the filter tool ("Filtri" button below the text) cannot be customized by the reader, but it is permanently active in order to display certain remarkable features. In the edited text (view mode "Testo di lettura"), colors indicate the reason (the @cause attribute) for specific readings, as follows:
grammatical: pink;
orthographic: blue. In each witness's text (view mode "Collazione"), the filters highlight (in brown) the points where a correction was made.
Occasional commentary on the readings is provided within the text itself (clickable red dots); further remarks can be found under the apparatus (right-hand panel of the screen "Testo di lettura"), either by clicking on "Nota critica" or by accessing the XML structure (when the information refers to a single witness and is therefore embedded in a witDetail element).
The point in the text of Iosue where the witnesses place the chapter number corresponding to each capitulum can be accessed either by clicking on the two-page icon next to each capitulum number, or by clicking on the "Passi paralleli" thumbnail (in the right-hand panel of the "Testo di lettura" screen).

rdgGrp.rdg: INCiPiuNT CAPituLa IN LIBRO IESU NAVE
INCiPiuNT CAPiTuLa IN LIBRO IESU NAVE
INCiPiT CAPIT CAPITULATIO LIBri IESU NaVe

app.rdg: INCiPit CAPITULATIO IN LIBRO IESUM NAVE

app.witDetail: Due to the tight binding of the manuscript and the poor quality of the digitisation, the number cannot be read.

item.title:
Promittit deus iosue dicens sicut fui cum moyse moysi ero et
et tecum

app.witDetail: Due to the tight binding of the manuscript and the poor quality of the digitisation, the number cannot be read.

item.title:
Praeparare prȩparare praepara praeparare preparare cibaria iubetur populo ut transeant transeat transeant transeat
iordanem iordanen

app.witDetail: Due to the tight binding of the manuscript and the poor quality of the digitisation, the number cannot be read.

item.title: Destinantur exploratores explaratores a iosue in hiericho

app.witDetail: Due to the tight binding of the manuscript and the poor quality of the digitisation, the number cannot be read.

item.title:
Promittit dominus deus dominus iosue dicens hodie incipiam exaltare exlatare exaltare te coram omni israhel ut sciant quia The Vulgate (Ios 3:7) reads here quod instead of quia (no variant is recorded in Weber-Gryson's apparatus). Φ, Am2, de Bruyne vs Vulg. (adiaph.) sicut fui
Besides being attested in all our witnesses, the verb fui appears in the corresponding passage of the Vulgate (Ios 3:7: ut sciant quod sicut cum Mosi fui ita et tecum sim) and is certainly the original reading of the A series. Its absence in de Bruyne's edition may be due to a typographical slip.
Am2, Φ (= Vulg.) vs deBruyne cum moyse moysi moysi moyse ita et tecum sum Cf. app. Weber-Gryson ad Ios 3:7 (ita et tecum sim): sim] sum CΣT (C = Cava, Badia, 1 (14), 9th c. 2/2, Spain; Σ = Madrid, BN, Vitr. 13-1, 10th c., Spain; T = our Tu).

app.witDetail: Due to the tight binding of the manuscript and the poor quality of the digitisation, the number cannot be read.

item.title: Iubente domino siccauit alueus iordanis euntis The correct and expected reading is an ablative, and eunte is indeed attested in Spanish and Beneventan witnesses of the A series, as shown by the apparatus of the Benedictine edition (p. 20). Despite being clearly erroneous, euntis is widespread: in addition to the Tours Bibles and Am2, it also occurs in some later witnesses (cf. ibid.). Tu's version reads transeunte. israhel

app.witDetail: Due to the tight binding of the manuscript and the poor quality of the digitisation, the number cannot be read.

item.title:
Duodecim lapides electi de iordanis alueo ob o? ob commemorationem futuri
temporis

app.witDetail: Due to the tight binding of the manuscript and the poor quality of the digitisation, the number cannot be read.

item.title: Introeunte israhel terram chanaan territi sunt cuncti reges regis

app.witDetail: Due to the tight binding of the manuscript and the poor quality of the digitisation, the number cannot be read.

item.title: Confortat dominus iosue ad proeliandum praeliandum praedicandum

app.witDetail: Due to the tight binding of the manuscript and the poor quality of the digitisation, the number cannot be read.

item.title: Perturbatio regum
Simulatio gabaonitarum

app.witDetail: Due to the tight binding of the manuscript and the poor quality of the digitisation, the number cannot be read.

item.title:
Conglobati quinque vque reges contra iosue ascenderunt
XVIII tunc imperauit
soli et stetit spatium spacium spatio According to the apparatus of the Benedictine edition of the Vulgate, spatio, preferred by de Bruyne, occurs only in the Bible of San Paolo Fuori le Mura and is therefore most likely a peculiar innovation of that manuscript. Φ, Am2 vs de Bruyne
diei unius unius diei The episode described by the second part of the capitulum (tunc imperauit soli...) is narrated in Ios 10:12-14, despite the fact that in all the Turonian Bibles chapter 18 already begins at Ios 10:8.

app.witDetail: Due to the tight binding of the manuscript and the poor quality of the digitisation, the number cannot be read.

item.title: Iuuante domino extincti sunt reges cum exercitu suo

app.witDetail: Due to the tight binding of the manuscript and the poor quality of the digitisation, the number cannot be read.

item.title:
Ruben
et gad et dimidiae dimidiȩ dimediae dimidiæ dimedie dimediæ
tribui tribu manasse terram sibi
dari dare Despite the preference given to it by de Bruyne, dare is clearly inferior to dari. Its presence in our two oldest witnesses (Am2 and Sg1), if not accidental, may be noteworthy: in the Benedictine edition of the Vulgate, this same error is attested only in 11th- and 12th-century manuscripts. Φ (praeter Sg1) vs Sg1, Am2, deBruyne (error) postulantes
s
a moyse

app.witDetail: Due to the tight binding of the manuscript and the poor quality of the digitisation, the number cannot be read.

repetit reppetit
chaleb caleb chaleb montem in

adam situm
est

item.title: Sors iudae iudȩ iudæ
Sors filiorum filiorum filiorum ioseph ephraim effraim et manasse

app.witDetail: Due to the tight binding of the manuscript and the poor quality of the digitisation, the number cannot be read.

item.title:
Filii fillii ioseph petunt petent petant The presence of the erroneous petent in Tu (see separate edition), as in Am2, may suggest a relationship between the two witnesses, or at least indicate that it is an ancient error. Φ (praeter Z, B2) vs Z, Am2 [+ Tu] (error) sibi augmentari augmentarii funiculum a
a iosue quibus ait ascendite in
montem et succidite siluam et mundate uobis ad habitandum abitandum habitandum abitandum habitandum habitandam habitandum
spatia spacia

app.witDetail: Due to the tight binding of the manuscript and the poor quality of the digitisation, the number cannot be read.

app.lem: Congregatique sunt omnes filii israhel in silo
app.rdg: Congregatique sunt omnes filii israhel in silo
app.note: The capitulum reproduces Ios 18:1 so literally that the enclitic -que in congregatique is retained, even though it is superfluous in the context of the capitulum.

app.witDetail: Due to the tight binding of the manuscript and the poor quality of the digitisation, the number cannot be read.

item.title:
Destinantur uiri ad describendam discribendam describandam Φ, de Bruyne vs Am2 terram reuersique descriptionem discriptionem describtionem Φ, de Bruyne vs Am2 tradunt iosue qui misit sortes fortes sorte sortes coram domino in silo sylo
diuisitque diuisique diuisitque terram filiis israhel in septem partes

bibl.title: Iosue 20:1 loquere filiis (Am2, B2 [+ Tu]): 20:1 et locutus est dominus ad iosue dicens loquere filiis)

app.witDetail: Due to the tight binding of the manuscript and the poor quality of the digitisation, the number cannot be read.
The scribe of Sg1 writes fugitiur (with a macron above the r), likely employing a non-standard abbreviation due to a lack of space on the line.

bibl.title: Iosue 21:2 locutique sunt ad eos (B2: 21:2 dominus praecepit; Tu: 21:1 accesseruntque principes)

obitus sui

item.title: Unde natus sit abraham uel ubi perductus p??ductus perductus item congregatis cum pacto obtestationis adloquitur alloquitur eos iosue
